# Outreach Templates

Supporting reference for the `first-users` skill.

---

## The Rules Before Templating

Before using any template, customize the opener. Generic outreach fails because it looks
like spam. The first line must be about them, not you.

**Good first line (specific):**
> "Saw your post about struggling with client onboarding last week —"

**Bad first line (generic):**
> "Hi, I hope this message finds you well."

---

## DM Templates (Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Instagram)

### Template 1: Problem-first (B2B/professional)

```
Subject: Quick question about [their problem area]

Hi [name],

Saw [specific thing about them — post, article, profile detail].

I'm building [product] — [one sentence what it does]. Looking for 10 early users to
shape what we build.

Would you be up for trying it? I'd love your honest feedback.

[link]

[your name]
```

### Template 2: Direct feedback ask (consumer / prosumer)

```
Hi [name],

[Personal reference — why you're reaching out to them specifically].

I built [product] because [personal reason]. It's early, and I'm looking for people
who [experience the problem] to test it out.

If you have 15 minutes to try it and tell me what's broken, I'd really appreciate it.

[link]
```

### Template 3: Peer-to-peer (founder to founder)

```
Hey [name],

I've been following your [work/posts/company] — really like [specific thing].

I'm building [product] for [audience]. I know you work with a lot of [target user type],
and I'd love to get your reaction to it.

Worth a quick look?

[link]
```

---

## Cold Email Templates

### Template 1: Short cold email (under 100 words)

```
Subject: [Specific subject — reference their company/role/pain]

Hi [name],

[Personalized first line — reference something specific to them.]

I built [product] to help [target audience] [solve specific problem]. Early stage, but
working with [social proof if any — "5 companies like yours already"].

Worth 10 minutes of your time to take a look?

[link]

[your name]
[title / company]
```

### Template 2: Problem-led (slightly longer)

```
Subject: How [their company] handles [problem area]?

Hi [name],

[Personal reference — why you're reaching out to this person specifically.]

Most [target audience] I talk to [describe the painful workaround they use today].
I'm building something to fix this.

[Product] [one sentence description]. [Early traction / social proof if you have any.]

Would you be open to a quick call? I'm not selling — I want to understand how you
handle this today and get your reaction to what we're building.

[link to Calendly or direct ask for availability]

[your name]
```

### Template 3: One-line ask (hyper-short)

```
Subject: 15 minutes?

Hi [name],

I built [product] for [audience]. You seem like exactly the person who'd have a
strong opinion on it. 15 minutes to try it and tell me what's wrong?

[link]

[your name]
```

---

## Community Post Templates

### Reddit / Forum Post

```
Title: [Describe the problem or outcome — not a product pitch]

Hey [community name],

I've been [brief personal context — why this matters to you].

I built [product] to [solve X]. It's early and rough, but I'd love feedback from
people who actually [experience the problem].

Here's what it does:
- [Bullet 1 — benefit]
- [Bullet 2 — benefit]
- [Bullet 3 — benefit]

If you want to try it: [link]

Happy to answer questions. And if this doesn't solve the right problem — tell me that
too. Honest feedback beats polite silence.
```

**Rules for Reddit:**
- Match the subreddit tone — read 5-10 recent posts before posting
- Add value beyond the self-promo — share what you learned building it
- Don't post the same thing in multiple subreddits the same day
- Respond to every comment, even critical ones

### Indie Hackers / Hacker News Post

```
Title: I built [product] because [personal pain point] — here's what I learned

[Tell the story of building it — 2-3 paragraphs]

[What it does — 1 paragraph, specific and honest]

[What's working / not working — be transparent about the stage]

[Where to try it — link]

I'm looking for early users who [describe your ICP] to test and give honest feedback.
Would love to hear your reaction.
```

---

## Follow-Up Templates

### Follow-up 1 (3-5 days after initial outreach, no response)

```
Hi [name],

Just wanted to follow up on my message from [X] days ago.

I understand if it's not relevant — just want to make sure it didn't get lost.

[link]

[your name]
```

**Note:** One follow-up is enough. Two maximum. Don't spam.

### Follow-up after they signed up but aren't active

```
Hi [name],

Saw you signed up for [product] last week — thanks!

Noticed you haven't [done X] yet. Totally normal at this stage, but I want to make
sure you're getting value.

Is there anything confusing or broken? Happy to walk you through it in 10 minutes.

[Calendly link or "just reply here"]

[your name]
```

---

## Asking for Referrals

Use after a positive interaction (they've used the product and given positive feedback):

```
Hey [name],

Really glad [product] is helping with [thing they mentioned].

I'm trying to find 10 more people like you to test it. Is there anyone in your network
who [experiences the same problem]? A quick intro would mean a lot.

No worries if not — appreciate you being part of this either way.

[your name]
```

---

## Customization Checklist

Before sending any message:

- [ ] First line references something specific about them (not generic)
- [ ] Message is under 5 sentences (DM) or under 150 words (email)
- [ ] Asking for feedback, not a sale
- [ ] Link is included and works
- [ ] You have a way to follow up (email, DM thread, calendar link)
- [ ] Personalized to their audience type (developer / founder / marketer / consumer)

input.content:
# Launch Checklist

Supporting reference for the `launch-plan` skill.

---

## Full Pre-Launch Checklist

### 4 Weeks Before

**Audience building:**
- [ ] Start posting about what you're building (build in public — 3-5x/week)
- [ ] Set up email capture (waitlist page, landing page, or Twitter/LinkedIn profile link)
- [ ] Identify 20-50 people who would be perfect early users
- [ ] Start engaging with potential users on their platforms (comment, reply, help)

**Product:**
- [ ] Define the one metric that will tell you if the launch worked
- [ ] Set up basic analytics (Plausible, PostHog, or GA4)
- [ ] Define your MVP cut line — what must work vs. what can be fixed post-launch

---

### 2 Weeks Before

**Content prep:**
- [ ] Write launch post draft for primary platform
- [ ] Write 3-5 supporting social posts for launch week
- [ ] Create a short demo video or GIF (30-60 seconds is ideal)
- [ ] Draft email to your waitlist / existing contacts
- [ ] Write Product Hunt listing (if applicable): tagline, description, maker comment

**Product:**
- [ ] Run the full user flow end-to-end at least 3 times
- [ ] Have 3 people outside your team try the product and watch them
- [ ] Fix any flow-breaking issues (not cosmetic issues — only blockers)
- [ ] Test on mobile

**Community prep:**
- [ ] Join 2-3 communities where your audience is active
- [ ] Be helpful in those communities (2 weeks of non-promotional participation)
- [ ] Identify community moderators or leaders who might support your launch

---

### 1 Week Before

**Logistics:**
- [ ] Pick your launch date and time (for PH: Tue/Wed/Thu, 12:01 AM PT)
- [ ] Confirm 5-10 people who will support on launch day (upvote, comment, share)
- [ ] Brief supporters on what you need: "Check it out and leave a comment" (not "upvote")
- [ ] Schedule all social posts in advance

**Final checks:**
- [ ] Landing page live and tested (desktop + mobile)
- [ ] All links work (landing page, signup, demo)
- [ ] Email capture working and connected to your list
- [ ] You can handle a sudden spike (Vercel/Netlify scale automatically, good)
- [ ] Clear support/contact option for new users

---

### Day Before

- [ ] Finalize launch post — read it out loud to check for clarity
- [ ] Brief any co-founders, advisors, or team members
- [ ] Set aside the full day — be available to respond within 30 minutes
- [ ] Charge your laptop, set up your desk

---

## Launch Day Schedule

### Option A: Morning Launch (recommended for LinkedIn/Twitter)

```
7:00 AM  — Publish launch post on primary platform
7:15 AM  — Send email to waitlist / existing contacts
7:30 AM  — Post supporting content on secondary platforms
8:00 AM  — DM personal list of 20-50 target users
9:00 AM  — Check and respond to all comments, replies, DMs
10:00 AM — Share a "first hour" update: "X signups in the first hour — thank you"
12:00 PM — Midday check: respond to everything, share milestone if appropriate
3:00 PM  — Afternoon check: respond, share update
6:00 PM  — Evening recap post: "Here's what happened today"
9:00 PM  — Final check and response sweep
```

### Option B: Product Hunt Launch (12:01 AM PT)

```
12:01 AM — Schedule submission goes live (if scheduled in advance)
8:00 AM  — Post your maker comment immediately
8:30 AM  — Alert your 5-10 supporters to check it out and comment
9:00 AM  — Share PH link on all social channels
9:00 AM - 5:00 PM — Respond to every comment on PH within 30 minutes
6:00 PM  — Share update post: "We're [X]th on Product Hunt today"
```

---

## Post-Launch Checklist

### Day 1-2

- [ ] Follow up personally with everyone who signed up (a quick "Hey, welcome" message)
- [ ] Respond to all comments, reviews, and DMs
- [ ] Collect feedback in a single place (Notion, Airtable, spreadsheet)
- [ ] Note the top 3 pieces of feedback (not the loudest — the most common)
- [ ] Fix any critical bug or blocker that more than 2 users hit

### Day 3-4

- [ ] Post a "what I learned" update (authentic, specific, data-inclusive)
- [ ] Start implementing the #1 most-requested change
- [ ] Reach out to users who signed up but haven't done the core action
- [ ] Collect 2-3 testimonials from happy early users (ask directly)

### Day 5-7

- [ ] Share user stories or testimonials (with permission)
- [ ] Write and publish a launch retrospective post
- [ ] Review your success metric — did you hit it? What does it tell you?
- [ ] Plan the next 30 days

---

## Platform-Specific Tips

### LinkedIn

| Tip | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| Link placement | Post the link in the first comment, not the body |
| Best posting time | 8-10 AM Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday |
| Engagement window | Respond to every comment in the first 2 hours |
| Post length | 800-1200 characters performs well |
| Images | Native carousels and screenshots get more reach than external links |

### Twitter/X

| Tip | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| Thread format | First tweet is the hook — all the value |
| Quote tweeting | Reframe the thread from different angles throughout the day |
| Pinning | Pin the launch thread to your profile immediately |
| Follow-ups | Post "X signups in the first hour" updates — they get engagement |
| Hashtags | 1-2 max, relevant ones only |

### Product Hunt

| Tip | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| Launch day | Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday get the most traffic |
| Timing | 12:01 AM PT to maximize full day exposure |
| Tagline | 60 characters max, outcome-focused, no jargon |
| Screenshots | 5+ screenshots, show the core flow, not marketing images |
| Maker comment | Write a personal story — why you built it. This performs better than feature lists |
| Upvote rules | Don't ask directly for upvotes (against rules) — say "check it out and share feedback" |
| Supporting | Ask supporters to leave a comment, not just upvote |

### Reddit

| Tip | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| Participation | Be a community member for 2+ weeks before posting a product |
| Title | Frame as "I built X because Y" — not "Check out my product" |
| Cross-posting | Don't post the same thing in multiple subreddits the same day |
| Criticism | Respond to criticism gracefully — this wins community respect |
| AMAs | Consider doing an AMA if you get traction — "I spent 6 months building X, AMA" |

### Hacker News (Show HN)

| Tip | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| Title format | "Show HN: [Product] — [one-line description]" |
| Story | Lead with the interesting technical problem or insight |
| Authenticity | Be honest about limitations, stage, and what you don't know |
| Criticism | HN commenters are blunt — engage thoughtfully, not defensively |
| Timing | Post at 9 AM ET Monday-Wednesday for best visibility |

---

## Launch Metrics Tracker

```
LAUNCH METRICS
─────────────────────────────────────────
Launch date: [date]
Launch type: [soft/community/platform/PR]

TRAFFIC
─────────────────────
Visitors:
Signup rate (%):
New signups:
Primary source:

ENGAGEMENT (platform-specific)
─────────────────────
Upvotes / Likes:
Comments:
Shares / Reposts:
DMs received:

PRODUCT
─────────────────────
Active users (day 1):
Completed core action:
Feedback collected:
Bugs reported:

TOP FEEDBACK (most common)
─────────────────────
1.
2.
3.

NEXT ACTIONS
─────────────────────
□
□
□
```

input.command:
# Check description lengths with a simpler approach
for f in /Users/canerden/founder-skills/skills/*/SKILL.md; do
  skill=$(basename $(dirname $f))
  # Get lines 3-7 (description block), remove yaml key prefix
  desc=$(python3 -c "
import sys
lines = open('$f').readlines()
in_desc = False
desc = ''
for line in lines:
    if line.startswith('description:'):
        in_desc = True
        desc += line[len('description:'):].strip().strip('\"')
    elif in_desc and (line.startswith('  ') or line.startswith('\t')):
        desc += ' ' + line.strip().strip('\"')
    elif in_desc:
        break
print(len(desc), desc[:80])
")
  echo "$skill: $desc"
done
